prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetheart, a CPU is something people call the “brain” of the computer. You know how our brains tell our arms and legs what to do? Well, the CPU tells the computer what to do, like playing your games or opening your pictures. It’s a little square chip inside the computer box that does a lot of thinking really, really fast. I don’t know all the details, but without it, the computer wouldn’t really work at all. It’s like the boss that helps everything else know what job to do!
